name: Branford Marsalis
abstract: Branford Marsalis (born August 26, 1960) is an American saxophonist, composer, and bandleader. While primarily known for his work in jazz as the leader of the Branford Marsalis Quartet, he also performs frequently as a soloist with classical ensembles and has led the group Buckshot LeFonque.

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: Béla has enjoyed musical collaborations with artists such as ... Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.doc_title: Bela Fleck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 7, 2004

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Guys were starting to come out of the woodwork, Branford Marsalis, Rick Margitza, Brecker. Bob Berg changed his whole style. Everybody was playing this language, and I felt weird. ... It was weird to hear that stuff being shouted back to you in a jam session, and you knew they had heard it – not fro

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Branford Marsalis in the context of teaching double lip; INTERVIEWEE responds generally about the technique and sound, without indicating any personal interaction or specific relationship with Branford Marsalis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I just wanna let you know that, I don't know if you know this or not, but that was sampled by Branford Marsalis in a hip-hop album called Buckshot LeFonque

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: Branford Marsalis called me up to play this show with Sting. There were four horns... and there were about nine stars on the show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: I thanked all the people you’re supposed to thank — David Chertok, the film collector, Wynton Marsalis, Chick Corea, Branford
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012
